name: djongo
documentation:
Use Mongodb as a backend database for your django project, without changing a
single django model!

Usage
-----

1. Install djongo::

      pip install djongo

2. Into settings.py file of your project, add::

      DATABASES = {
           'default': {
               'ENGINE': 'djongo',
               'NAME': 'your-db-name',
           }
       }

3. Run (ONLY the first time to create collections in mongoDB)::

      manage.py makemigrations
      manage.py migrate

YOUR ARE SET! HAVE FUN!

Requirements
------------

1. Djongo requires python 3.6 or above.


How it works
------------

Djongo is a SQL to mongodb query transpiler. It translates a SQL query string
into a mongoDB query document. As a result, all Django features, models etc
work as is.

Django contrib modules::

    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.sessions',

and others... fully supported.

Important links
---------------

* `Full Documentation <https://www.djongomapper.com/>`_
* `Source code <https://github.com/doableware/djongo>`_

modules.name: djongo.models.indexes
documentation: This version of djongo does not support indexes

classes.name: djongo.sql2mongo.functions.DateTrunc
documentation: Allows to truncate datetime fields to any of their parts

modules.name: djongo.models.fields
documentation:
The standard way of using djongo is to import models.py
in place of Django's standard models module.

Djongo Fields is where custom fields for working with
MongoDB is defined.

 - EmbeddedField
 - ArrayField
 - ArrayReferenceField
 - GenericReferenceField

These are the main fields for working with MongoDB.

functions.name: djongo.models.fields.make_mdl
documentation: Builds an instance of model from the model_dict.

classes.name: djongo.models.fields.DjongoManager
documentation:
This modified manager allows to issue Mongo functions by prefixing
them with 'mongo_'.

This module allows methods to be passed directly to pymongo.

classes.name: djongo.models.fields.ModelField

methods.name: djongo.models.fields.ModelField.to_python
documentation:
Overrides Django's default to_python to allow correct
translation to instance.

Allows for the inclusion of an instance of an abstract model as
a field inside a document.

classes.name: djongo.models.fields.ArrayField
documentation:
Implements an array of objects inside the document.

The allowed object type is defined on model declaration. The
declared instance will accept a python list of instances of the
given model as its contents.

The model of the container must be declared as abstract, thus should
not be treated as a collection of its own.

methods.name: djongo.models.fields.EmbeddedFormBoundField.__str__
documentation: def __getitem__(self, name):
return self.field.model_form[name]
def __getattr__(self, name):
return getattr(self.field.model_form, name)

classes.name: djongo.models.fields.ObjectIdField
documentation:
For every document inserted into a collection MongoDB internally creates an field.
The field can be referenced from within the Model as _id.

methods.name: djongo.models.fields.ArrayReferenceManagerMixin._apply_rel_filters
documentation: Filter the queryset for the instance this manager is bound to.

methods.name: djongo.models.fields.ArrayReferenceDescriptor.__get__
documentation:
Get the related objects through the reverse relation.

With the example above, when getting `parent.children`:

- `self` is the descriptor managing the `children` attribute
- `instance` is the `parent` instance
- `cls` is the `Parent` class (unused)

methods.name: djongo.models.fields.ReverseArrayReferenceDescriptor.__get__
documentation:
Get the related objects through the relation.

With the example above, when getting `parent.children`:

- `self` is the descriptor managing the `children` attribute
- `instance` is the `parent` instance
- `cls` is the `Parent` class (unused)

classes.name: djongo.models.fields.ArrayReferenceField
documentation:
When the entry gets saved, only a reference to the primary_key of the model is saved in the array.
For all practical aspects, the ArrayReferenceField behaves like a Django ManyToManyField.
class ArrayManyToManyField(ManyToManyField):
def contribute_to_class(self, cls, name, **kwargs):
super().contribute_to_class(cls, name, **kwargs)
setattr(cls, self.name, ArrayManyToManyDescriptor(self.remote_field, reverse=False))

modules.name: djongo.sql2mongo.query
documentation:
Module with constants and mappings to build MongoDB queries from
SQL constructors.

methods.name: djongo.introspection.DatabaseIntrospection.get_table_list
documentation: def table_names(self, cursor=None, include_views=False):
return sorted(cursor.m_cli_connection.collection_names(False))

modules.name: djongo.base
documentation: MongoDB database backend for Django

classes.name: djongo.base.DatabaseWrapper

methods.name: djongo.base.DatabaseWrapper.get_connection_params
documentation:
Default method to acquire database connection parameters.

Sets connection parameters to match settings.py, and sets
default values to blank fields.

methods.name: djongo.base.DatabaseWrapper.get_new_connection
documentation:
Receives a dictionary connection_params to setup
a connection to the database.

Dictionary correct setup is made through the
get_connection_params method.

methods.name: djongo.base.DatabaseWrapper._set_autocommit
documentation:
Default method must be overridden, eventhough not used.

TODO: For future reference, setting two phase commits and rollbacks
might require populating this method.

methods.name: djongo.base.DatabaseWrapper.create_cursor
documentation: Returns an active connection cursor to the database.

methods.name: djongo.base.DatabaseWrapper._close
documentation: Closes the client connection to the database.

methods.name: djongo.base.DatabaseWrapper._commit
documentation:
Commit routine

TODO: two phase commits are not supported yet.
DatabaseWrapper for MongoDB using SQL replacements.

functions.name: djongo.storage._get_subcollections
documentation: Returns all sub-collections of `collection`.

classes.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage._open
documentation:
Returns a {class}`~gridfs.GridOut` file opened in `mode`, or
raises {exc}`~gridfs.errors.NoFile` if the requested file
doesn't exist and mode is not 'w'.

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage._save
documentation: Saves `content` into the file at `path`.

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage.delete
documentation: Deletes the file at `path` if it exists.

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage.exists
documentation: Returns `True` if the file at `path` exists in GridFS.

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage.listdir
documentation:
Returns a tuple (folders, lists) that are contained in the
folder `path`.

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage.size
documentation: Returns the size of the file at `path`.

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage.created_time
documentation: Returns the datetime the file at `path` was created.

methods.name: djongo.storage.GridFSStorage._get_gridfs
documentation:
Returns a {class}`~gridfs.GridFS` using the sub-collection for
`path`.

GridFS Storage backend for Django.
Based on <https://github.com/django-nonrel/mongodb-engine/blob/master/django_mongodb_engine/storage.py>

This backend aims to add a GridFS storage to upload files to
using Django's file fields.

For performance, the file hierarchy is represented as a tree of
MongoDB sub-collections.

(One could use a flat list, but to list a directory '/this/path/'
we would have to execute a search over the whole collection and
then filter the results to exclude those not starting by
'/this/path' using that model.)

```
param location

(optional) Name of the top-level node that holds the files. This
value of  is prepended to all file paths, so it works
like the  setting for Django's built-in
.

param collection

Name of the collection the file tree shall be stored in.
Defaults to 'storage'.

param database

Alias of the Django database to use. Defaults to 'default' (the
default Django database).

param base_url

URL that serves the files in GridFS (for instance, through
nginx-gridfs).
Defaults to None (file not accessible through a URL).
```

methods.name: djongo.operations.DatabaseOperations.date_extract_sql
documentation:
Given a lookup_type of 'year', 'month', or 'day', return the SQL that
extracts a value from the given date field field_name.

methods.name: djongo.operations.DatabaseOperations.datetime_extract_sql
documentation:
Given a lookup_type of 'year', 'month', or 'day', return the SQL that
extracts a value from the given date field field_name.
